'''Chris Hankinson''' (born 30 November 1993) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].

He has also played for [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Swinton Lions]], [[London Broncos]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and [[2016 League 1|League 1]], and [[Swinton Lions]] and [[London Broncos]] in the Championship. Hankinson has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]]. He has also played for [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]].

==Background==
Hankinson was born in [[Wigan]], Greater Manchester, England.

He is a qualified [[electrician]] and [[personal trainer]].{{cn|date=August 2023}}

==Career==
===Early career===
Hankinson played his junior at the Hindley ARLFC. He was in the junior systems of the [[Salford City Reds]], but did not progress through to the first team.{{cn|date=January 2023}}

===Leigh Centurions===
He left Salford to begin his professional career at the [[Leigh Centurions]]. He made his debut for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in 2014.

He was sent on loan by the [[Leigh Centurions|Centurions]] to the [[Barrow Raiders]], playing for the Cumbrian club throughout the [[2015 Championship 1]] season.

Leigh sent him to the [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] the following year, spending the entire [[2016 League 1]] season in [[Cumbria]].{{cn|date=January 2023}}

===Swinton Lions===
Hanksinson moved from Leigh to the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] ahead of the 2017 season.{{cn|date=January 2023}}
[[File:Chris Hankinson.jpg|thumb|right|400px|Hankinson playing for the Swinton Lions in 2017]]

===Wigan Warriors===
He moved to his hometown club in July 2018, and he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the derby against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

He spent part of 2019 on loan at his former club [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], featuring in eight matches in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]].
[[File:Chris Hankinson Magic Weekend 2019.jpg|thumb|right|Hankinson taking the field for Wigan at [[Anfield]] in May 2019]]
He won a League Leaders medal with Wigan in [[Super League XXV#Regular season table|2020]].

===London Broncos===
On 31 December 2020 it was announced that he had signed for the [[London Broncos]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=31 Dec 2020|accessdate=31 Dec 2020|title=London Broncos sign Wigan centre Chris Hankinson and Manly winger Abbas Miski|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55500952}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 9 November it was announced that Hankinson had left Wigan and signed for Toulouse Olympique on a two-year deal. He will become the third goal-kicker at Toulouse alongside [[Mark Kheirallah]] and [[Anthony Marion]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/toulouse-olympique-confirm-signing-from-wigan-warriors/|title = Toulouse Olympique confirm signing from Wigan Warriors|date = 9 November 2021}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 17 October 2022 it was announced that Hankinson had signed for Featherstone for the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web|website=Love Rugby League|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/chris-hankinson-toulouse-featherstone-championship/|title =Chris Hankinson departs Toulouse for Championship rivals|date =17 Oct 2022|accessdate=25 Oct 2022}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 8 December 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total Rugby League|date=8 Dec 2023|title=Salford Red Devils snap up Featherstone Rovers centre Chris Hankinson|url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-snap-up-featherstone-rovers-centre-chris-hankinson/}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
On 4 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Aug 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Leeds Rhinos complete shock deal for Salford star as exodus continues|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-rhinos-transfer-salford-red-devils-chris-hankinson-super-league}}</ref>

On 14 November 2025 [[Leeds Rhinos]] announced that he had been handed another 1-year contract to continue at the club for the 2026 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Nov 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Leeds Rhinos hand permanent deal to Salford Red Devils star as exodus continues|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-rhinos-hand-permanent-deal-to-salford-red-devils-star-as-exodus-continues}}</ref>
